source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Three circles $C_{1}, C_{2}$ and $C_{3}$ of radii $1 \mathrm{~cm}, 2 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $3 \mathrm{~cm}$ respectively touch as shown. $C_{1}$ meets $C_{2}$ at $P$ and meets $C_{3}$ at $Q$. What is the length in centimetres of the longer arc of circle $C_{1}$ between $P$ and $Q$ ? <image1>

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'touch as shown', so the configuration depends on the diagram
the asked arc on C1 between P and Q depends on spatial placement of tangencies/intersections shown in the image
multi_step: solving requires combining multiple geometric relations: circle tangencies/intersections, center-distance reasoning, then converting the resulting central angle into arc length
the target is the longer arc, which adds a second-stage selection after determining the angle between P and Q
multiple-choice options suggest a determinate geometric computation rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a specific exact expression: '\frac{3 \pi}{2}'
choices are provided and include the stated answer, enabling straightforward answer checking
the problem is a standard geometry quantity question with a unique numeric/symbolic target

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, and asset registry passed with no reported issues
high image dependency makes this a strong visually grounded sample
